input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Mu Ye Gui Shi | Ghost Blows Out the Light: Mu Ye Gui Shi () is a 2017 Chinese web series adapted from the novel series "Ghost Blows Out the Light" by Zhang Muye. Zhang also acts as the series' executive producer. The series is currently airing via iQiyi every Monday and Tuesday at 20:00 (CST) starting 3 July 2017.

Blow It Out (Ludacris song) | "Blow It Out" also known as "Blow It Out Yo Ass" is the third single by Ludacris from the album "Chicken-n-Beer". The diss song targets Bill O'Reilly because of the comments he made towards Ludacris.
Blow It Out (album) | Blow It Out is a jazz/fusion album released in 1977 by Tom Scott.

Glassblowing | Glassblowing is a glassforming technique that involves inflating molten glass into a bubble (or parison), with the aid of a blowpipe (or blow tube). A person who blows glass is called a glassblower, glassmith, or gaffer. A lampworker (often also called a glassblower or glassworker) manipulates glass with the use of a torch on a smaller scale, such as in producing precision laboratory glassware out of borosilicate glass.

Stump blow-out | Stump blow-out, or duodenal blow-out, is the leakage of the blind end of the duodenum. It occurs as a complication of Billroth II gastrectomy, usually on the fourth or fifth day after surgery. It is due to improper closure of duodenal stump, especially when the duodenum is inflamed and oedematous. It can also occur because of afferent loop block, local pancreatitis and distal obstruction.
The Blow Out | The Blow Out is a 1936 Looney Tunes animated short film starring Porky Pig. It was directed by Tex Avery.
Blow Your Face Out | Blow Your Face Out is the eighth album (and second live album) by American rock band The J. Geils Band, released in 1976.

output.query: George Litto Who was the producer of the 1981 American neo-noir political thriller film?

retrieved_doc_snippets: George Litto | George Litto is an American film producer and talent agent. His production credits include Robert Altman's "Thieves Like Us" (1974), Jonathan Kaplan's cult film "Over the Edge" (1979), and three Brian De Palma thrillers, "Obsession" (1976), "Dressed to Kill" (1980) and" Blow Out" (1981).

The Manchurian Candidate (1962 film) | The Manchurian Candidate is a 1962 American black and white neo-noir Cold War suspense thriller film scripted by George Axelrod, from the 1959 Richard Condon novel "The Manchurian Candidate", produced by Axelrod and John Frankenheimer, directed by Frankenheimer, and starring Frank Sinatra, Laurence Harvey, and Janet Leigh; co-starring are Angela Lansbury, Henry Silva, and James Gregory. "The Manchurian Candidate" concerns the brainwashing of the son of a pr ...[truncated]

Taskara | Taskara is a 2016 Telugu, neo-noir cyber heist Geo political thriller film written by Kirti Rambhatla. The film is directed by a team at LME, music by Rohit Kumar, and produced by Libra Media & Entertainment Motion Pictures.

Blow Out | Blow Out is a 1981 American neo-noir political thriller film written and directed by Brian De Palma. The film stars John Travolta as Jack Terry, a movie sound effects technician from Philadelphia who, while recording sounds for a low-budget slasher film, serendipitously captures audio evidence of an assassination involving a presidential hopeful. Nancy Allen stars as Sally Bedina, a young woman involved in the crime. The supporting cast includes John Lithgow and Dennis Franz. The film ...[truncated]

Shanghai (2010 film) | Shanghai is a 2010 American neo-noir/political thriller film directed by Mikael Håfström, starring John Cusack and Gong Li. The film was released in China on June 17, 2010. The film was released in the United States on October 2, 2015, in a limited release.

Nick of Time (film) | Nick of Time is a 1995 American neo noir political thriller film, directed by John Badham, and starring Johnny Depp, Christopher Walken, Charles S. Dutton, and Courtney Chase. It is noteworthy for taking place in real time.

Memento (film) | Memento is a 2000 American neo-noir psychological thriller film directed and written by Christopher Nolan, and produced by Suzanne and Jennifer Todd. The film's script was based on a pitch by Jonathan Nolan, who later wrote the story "Memento Mori" from the concept. It stars Guy Pearce, Carrie-Anne Moss, and Joe Pantoliano.
target_state.summary_2: The producer of the 1981 American neo-noir political thriller Blow Out was George Litto. George Litto is an American film producer and talent agent whose production credits include Brian De Palma's Blow Out (1981).

}

- Δp_i is a PROMPT EDIT / behavior rule, never the target output.

- Sample-specific anchors may appear only when they already occur in the
  visible agent inputs below.

- Preserve the agent's original input/output interface.
